principle: Every page must have persistent navigation with four elements — logo linking home in top-left, primary nav sections, search input, and utilities like login and cart
explanation: X.ux|persistent_nav: every_page_must_show: logo_top_left (clickable_to_home) + primary_nav (site_sections) + search_input (visible_field_not_hidden) + utilities (login/cart/help); same_position_same_look_on_every_page -> user_learns_once; exception: checkout_and_forms -> remove_nav_to_prevent_distraction_from_task_completion; NO:pages_without_navigation NO:logo_not_linking_to_home NO:nav_changing_position_between_pages eg: "some_pages_have_nav_some_dont" >> "consistent_nav_everywhere_except_checkout"

principle: Navigation labels must reveal site content — use specific category names that tell users what they will find, not abstract generic words
explanation: X.ux|nav_reveals_content: nav == table_of_contents_for_entire_site; user_glances_at_nav -> understands_site_scope_in_seconds; labels_must_be: specific_content_categories NOT abstract_words; "Sneakers+Boots+Sandals" >> "Products"; "Pricing+Docs+Blog" >> "Resources"; showing_what_exists == equally_important_as_helping_navigate; NO:generic_labels ("Solutions"/"Services"/"Resources") NO:one_word_covering_everything NO:internal_jargon eg: "Products | Solutions | Resources" >> "Running_Shoes | Training_Gear | Sale"

principle: Highlight the current section in navigation and add breadcrumbs on every inner page so users always know where they are
explanation: X.ux|you_are_here: user_must_instantly_see_current_location; two_mechanisms: active_nav_item (bold/color/underline_indicating_current_section) + breadcrumbs (on_every_inner_page); breadcrumb_rules: separator_">" + last_item_bold_not_a_link + small_font + top_of_content_area; web_has_no_physical_cues -> users_feel_lost_without_location_indicators; NO:nav_without_active_state NO:breadcrumbs_where_last_item_is_clickable NO:inner_pages_without_location_context eg: "nav_all_items_same_style" >> "current_section_highlighted + breadcrumb_Home>Category>Current_Page"

principle: Always provide both search input and browse navigation equally visible — half of users search first and half browse first, hiding either path loses half your audience
explanation: X.ux|search_and_browse: search_dominant_users -> go_straight_to_search_box (like_asking_store_clerk); link_dominant_users -> click_nav_links_first (like_walking_aisles); both_types == roughly_equal; generator_must: visible_search_field + clear_nav_links_side_by_side; content_heavy_sites: search_field_always_expanded_not_behind_icon; NO:search_hidden_behind_magnifying_glass_icon_on_content_sites NO:nav_only_without_search NO:search_only_without_browse_navigation eg: "search_icon_only_no_nav" >> "expanded_search_field + full_nav_bar_both_in_header"
